decks.notes: This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan with a combination of hand disruption spells like Thoughtseize and counterspells like Force of Will, while applying pressure with efficient creatures like Orcish Bowmasters and Barrowgoyf. The deck aims to control the board through removal spells like Fatal Push and tempo plays with cards like Daze, all while maintaining card advantage and selection through cantrips like Brainstorm and Ponder. Additionally, powerful threats like Murktide Regent and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares provide a quick clock to close out the game.

The deck focuses on establishing a lock on the opponent by using artifact cards to control the game. It utilizes cards like Trinisphere and Planar Nexus to slow down the opponent's gameplay, while also using Urza's Tower, Mine, and Power Plant to generate mana for powerful artifacts like Ugin, Eye of the Storms and The One Ring. Cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Disruptor Flute help disrupt the opponent's strategy, ultimately aiming to establish control over the game and win through big threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn or Cityscape Leveler.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly and efficiently mill through the entire library, utilizing cards like Balustrade Spy and Undercity Informer to put key pieces like Thassa's Oracle or Dread Return into the graveyard. By using efficient spells like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal, as well as mana accelerators like Simian Spirit Guide and Chrome Mox, the deck aims to win in a single turn by either comboing off with Thassa's Oracle or reanimating key creatures from the graveyard. The deck is all in on its combo, risking it all for a quick and explosive win.

The main idea of this deck is to create a lock-down prison using artifacts and control elements. By utilizing cards like Urza's Tower, Karn, the Great Creator, and Trinisphere, the deck aims to restrict the opponent's options and prevent them from developing their board. The inclusion of powerful artifacts like Candelabra of Tawnos and Ugin, Eye of the Storms adds to the control strategy, while cards like Grim Monolith and Disruptor Flute provide additional utility and disruption. Overall, the deck focuses on utilizing artifacts to create a powerful prison that limits the opponent's ability to play their cards effectively.

This Legacy deck revolves around quickly and efficiently getting powerful creatures onto the battlefield using cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. By utilizing these cards alongside powerful spells like Omniscience and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with massive creatures and win the game in just a few turns. The deck also includes key protection spells like Force of Will and Veil of Summer to ensure its combo goes off successfully. Overall, the deck's main strategy is to cheat large creatures into play and quickly close out the game.

The main concept of this Legacy Control - Stoneblade deck is to use efficient removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Prismatic Ending, along with disruption cards like Force of Will and Counterbalance, to control the game and outvalue the opponent. The deck also aims to efficiently tutor and play powerful equipment like Batterskull and Kaldra Compleat using Stoneforge Mystic. Jace, the Mind Sculptor and Teferi, Time Raveler provide additional card advantage and utility, while cards like Snapcaster Mage and Ponder help dig for answers and threats. The deck also includes a mana denial element with Back to Basics.

The deck focuses on using control elements such as counterspells, removal, and card draw to disrupt the opponent's game plan and protect its own win conditions. It utilizes powerful planeswalkers like Tamiyo and Teferi, along with efficient counterspells like Force of Will and Stifle, to control the pace of the game. The deck also includes versatile removal options like Swords to Plowshares and Consign to Memory. By effectively managing resources and controlling the board, this UWx Control deck aims to outmaneuver opponents and win through attrition and powerful threats.

The deck aims to control the game by using a variety of counterspells and removal spells to handle any threats from the opponent. It also utilizes card draw and manipulation to ensure consistency in finding the necessary answers. The inclusion of planeswalkers like Tamiyo and Teferi provide additional value and disruption to the opponent's game plan. The deck has a strong mana base with fetch lands and dual lands to support the mana requirements of the spells. Overall, it is a versatile and adaptable control deck designed to outlast and outmaneuver opponents.

The main focus of this UR Tempo deck is to quickly play aggressive threats like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent while controlling the game with countermagic like Daze and Force of Will. The deck also utilizes cards like Lightning Bolt and Wasteland for removal and disruption. By efficiently deploying threats and disrupting the opponent's game plan, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent and win the game before they can stabilize. The deck's tempo-oriented strategy allows it to take advantage of the opponent's missteps and outmaneuver them to secure victory.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying disruptive creatures like Magus of the Moon and Hexing Squelcher, while using Chalice of the Void and Blood Moon to lock opponents out of their spells. The deck also utilizes powerful burn spells like Fury and Sundering Eruption to finish off opponents quickly. The mana base leans heavily on City of Traitors and Ancient Tomb for fast mana production, allowing for early plays of big threats like Pyrogoyf. The overall strategy is to establish board control and pressure opponents with aggressive plays, ultimately overwhelming them before they can stabilize.

The main concept of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's strategy while putting pressure on them with aggressive creatures and efficient removal spells. By utilizing cards like Thoughtseize, Fatal Push, and Force of Will, the deck aims to control the board and tempo of the game. Additionally, utilizing cards like Barrowgoyf and Orcish Bowmasters to apply pressure and close out the game quickly. The deck also includes cards like Brainstorm and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student to provide card advantage and maintain consistency throughout the game.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Archon of Cruelty, then using reanimation spells such as Reanimate and Animate Dead to bring them back into play for a devastating combo. Thoughtseize and Cabal Therapy provide disruption for the opponent's strategy, while cards like Collective Brutality and Raph & Mikey, Troublemakers offer additional utility and control options. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with huge creatures early in the game and secure a victory before they can mount a defense.

The deck aims to quickly cheat powerful creatures like Emrakul and Archon of Cruelty onto the battlefield using cards like Show and Tell, Sneak Attack, and Omniscience. It also includes cards to protect these combo pieces, such as Flusterstorm and Force of Will. The deck seeks to overwhelm the opponent with big creatures and powerful abilities, while using cards like Ponder and Brainstorm to manipulate draws and find key pieces. The goal is to win the game in a few turns by quickly putting together devastating combos.

This Eldrazi Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying powerful Eldrazi creatures and disrupting the opponent with cards like Chalice of the Void and Thought-Knot Seer. It aims to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and efficient mana sources like Lotus Petal and Ancient Tomb. The deck also includes land destruction with Wasteland to control the opponent's resources. It utilizes a mix of Eldrazi creatures with varying abilities to create a versatile and powerful aggressive strategy. The key is to establish a fast board presence and disrupt the opponent's game plan to secure a swift victory.

The deck focuses on locking down opponents with a combination of artifact-based control elements such as Trinisphere and Karn, the Great Creator, supported by powerful mana acceleration from cards like Grim Monolith and Urza's lands. The goal is to establish a powerful board presence with artifacts like The One Ring and Candelabra of Tawnos while disrupting opponent's game plans with cards like Pithing Needle and Kozilek's Command. The inclusion of cards like Planar Nexus and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn provide additional win conditions, making it difficult for opponents to overcome this prison-style control strategy.

The deck focuses on control elements and utilizing Stoneforge Mystic to search for equipment such as Batterskull. The key strategy involves disrupting the opponent's plays with counterspells like Force of Will and Stifle, while also generating value through cards like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd. The deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents with a combination of removal, card draw, and equipment to establish control of the game and close out matches efficiently. It also utilizes land disruption with Wasteland to keep opponents off key resources.
